title: Aquarium
name: Aquarium
intro: Aquarium is a water-filling logic puzzle played on a grid divided into blocks called aquariums. The below puzzle has 9 regions.
goal: Your goal is to fill aquariums with water so that they meet certain conditions.
board_example: Below is an empty Aquarium puzzle:
rule_gravity: Water obeys gravity: Within each aquarium, if a cell contains water, all cells below it in the same aquarium must also contain water.
rule_gravity_invalid: The water placement on the left is incorrect.
rule_gravity_valid: The right board is correct because all water cells below the highest level are filled.
rule_independent: Aquariums are independent: Different aquariums can have different water levels.
rule_independent_example: Below, the left aquarium is filled 2 water cells high, while the aquarium adjacent is filled completely. This is valid placement.
rule_hints: Row & Column hints: The numbers along the top and left edges indicate how many cells in that row or column must be filled with water.
rule_hints_example: In the example below, both the left and right boards are considered valid bottom row placements.
solved: A puzzle is solved when all row and column counts are satisfied and water obeys gravity within each aquarium.
control_cycle: Click a cell to cycle through states:

compact.intro: Fill some cells with water so that:
compact.rule_hints: Numbers show how many water cells per row/column
compact.rule_gravity: Water fills from bottom up within each aquarium
